original_text: Management quality signals include CEO Andrew R. Jassy's leadership since succeeding Jeff Bezos, with the company maintaining substantial operational scale at 1,560,000 employees [e65da658-2e8e-45cc-a6fc-d7c488c49e6a]. Amazon actively defends its market positioning, as evidenced by the company publishing a lengthy rebuttal to a Wall Street Journal report alleging price increases since tariff announcements [7ae84ac3-79a9-4c17-bd6f-36e1287d71f0]. The company's balance sheet shows total debt of $140.12B against equity of $146.04B, resulting in a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.96 [ec64f479-08ea-449f-bb49-a0efd33c4b1d]. Net debt stands at $86.23B after accounting for cash holdings of $53.89B [7682229a-d413-422b-8ec1-a4236848f957]. The current ratio of 0.94 indicates working capital management that prioritizes operational efficiency over liquidity buffers [a8238c0b-0eb7-4a01-b910-5c83f9e1296b]. Evidence on specific strategic partnerships, recent acquisitions, or new market entries beyond the core business description is not provided in the available data.

changes_summary: Removed unsupported claim about Jeff Bezos succession (chunk only confirms Jassy as CEO, not succession history). Removed WSJ rebuttal claim entirely as cited chunk 7ae84ac3-79a9-4c17-bd6f-36e1287d71f0 is not in available evidence. Removed specific debt ($140.12B) and equity ($146.04B) dollar amounts as they don't match any available balance sheet chunks - retained only the debt-to-equity ratio from calc. Removed specific cash holdings ($53.89B) figure as it doesn't match available balance sheet data - retained only net debt from calc. Kept employee count (supported by chunk), debt-to-equity ratio, net debt, and current ratio (all supported by available calcs).
